title: Avoid setting fields to null in finalize method
description: This rule raises an issue when a finalizer assigns null to fields of the instance it is called on. In the Java object lifecycle, the finalize method for an instance is called after the garbage collector has determined that the instance can be removed from the object heap. Therefore, it is unnecessary to implement a finalizer to set instance fields explicitly to null to tell the garbage collector that the instance no longer needs them.
recommendation: Remove the null assignment in the finalize method.
impact: In the worst case, implementing finalize is even counterproductive because it might accidentally create new references from other (living) objects on the heap to the collectible instance, thus, reviving it.

checks.message: Remove the null assignment in the finalize method.
